creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
One of my top Thailand travel tips 👇

Pre-book transport in advance from home ✔

Thailand is one of the easiest countries to travel around as a tourist, but of course, there will always be a few dodgy transport operators who overcharge or provide bad service. 

For a travel planning geek like myself, pre-booking transfer tickets in advance and reading reviews by fellow travellers have made me super confident even when I’m travelling around remote parts of Southeast Asia 😎

The downside of booking tickets online is that the price can sometimes be a bit lower if you book “in person” at a local tour company ticket booth if you are, for example, in one of the popular tourist towns like Ao Nang in Krabi or Patong in Phuket 🤓
 My personal opinion is that I’d much rather pay a little bit extra and know that I’m travelling with a reliable company where everything just works and feels safe 😇  Would you try and get the lowest possible price and “not know” or do you prefer to pre-book online at a site where you can check reviews? Let me know in the comments ✌️  #thailandtravel #thailandtrip #amazingthailand

transcription.text: Here's the secret to not having terrible travel days when you're in Thailand. I keep seeing so many people on here complaining that their travel days in Thailand are absolutely horrible so I'll stop the gatekeeping and let you in on a little secret. And no, this is not a sponsored post. I've actually been using this website myself on all my Thailand trips and it's just a lifesaver. So people seem to think that you have to book all your Thailand transportation live on location as if the internet hasn't made its way to Southeast Asia yet, when actually you can plan it all from the comfort of your own home if you just head over to 12GoAsia, put in your details and then you can see all the different transport options. You can book tickets and more importantly you can read reviews of the operators so you don't end up on a shitty overcrowded bus or boat.
